SEARCH
Module com.example.simul2d
Package com.example.simul2d.Core

Class SimulationState

java.lang.Object
com.example.simul2d.Core.SimulationState
public class SimulationState extends Object
Encapsulates the mutable state of the simulation.

This class stores the simulation-wide data such as the Grid, current time and execution speed. It also exposes a ReadWriteLock which callers can use to obtain thread-safe access to the state from different threads (for example the simulation thread and UI threads).

  • Constructor Summary

    Description

    Creates a default simulation state.

  • Constructor Details

    • SimulationState

      public SimulationState()
      Creates a default simulation state.

  • Method Details

    • changePause

      public void changePause()
      Toggles the paused flag.

      Convenience method to flip between paused and running states.

    • changePause

      public void changePause(boolean paused)

    • setSpeed

      public void setSpeed(double speed)
      Sets the simulation speed when the provided value is positive.
      Parameters:
      speed - the new speed value (must be > 0 to take effect)

    • setGrid

      public void setGrid(Grid grid)

    • getLock

      public ReadWriteLock getLock()

    • getGrid

      public Grid getGrid()

    • updateGridSnapshot

      public void updateGridSnapshot()
      Publishes an atomic string snapshot of the grid for UI consumption.

      Call this method from the simulation thread after mutating the grid to produce a textual representation which UI threads can read without acquiring the state lock.

    • getGridSnapshot

      public String getGridSnapshot()

      If no snapshot has been published yet this method will fall back to returning the current Grid.toString() value.

      Returns:
      a textual representation of the grid suitable for display

    • isPaused

      public boolean isPaused()
      Returns:
      true when paused

    • getSpeed

      public double getSpeed()
      Returns:
      the speed value used to control loop timing

    • getTime

      public double getTime()
      Returns:
      the elapsed time (in simulation steps)

    • resetSimulation

      public void resetSimulation()

    • addTime

      public void addTime(double dt)
      Adds the given delta time to the simulation clock.
      Parameters:
      dt - the time increment to add (non-negative values will advance time)
